name: Pool of Arches
description: You can row a boat through this ancient underground reservoir.

name: Rosh HaNikra Grottoes
description: The bluer than blue waters in these natural Israeli caves are reached via the world's steepest cable car.

name: Beit Guvrin Caves
description: These numerous Israeli caves were dug out over hundreds years for reasons ranging from hideouts to burial sites.

name: Razzouk Ink
description: This family tattoo parlor has been inking religious pilgrims in the Middle East for 700 years.

name: Shrine of the Book
description: The home of the Dead Sea Scrolls is an abstract modernist dream.

name: Western Wall Tunnel
description: Descend 2,030 years into the past in an ancient tunnel built to support Jerusalem's most famous wall.

name: Masada
description: This daunting 1st century fortress was purportedly the site of one of the most dramatic revolts of the Jewish-Roman wars.

name: Avshalom Cave
description: This amazing ancient cave was kept secret for seven years to protect it from the public.

name: Wadi Qelt
description: A serene desert oasis speckled with ancient artifacts.

name: Gan HaGat (Wine Press Garden)
description: A 2,000-year-old wine press hidden in an overlooked park in Tel Aviv.

name: Immovable Ladder on the Church of the Holy Sepulchre
description: A "miracle" that seems to produce more holy fistfights than holy revelations.

name: Zedekiah's Cave
description: An ancient quarry rediscovered by a dog and claimed by biblical kings, a German cult, and the Freemasons.

name: Red Canyon
description: Squeezing inside this deep, narrow canyon reveals a festival of desert colors.

name: Ein Avdat
description: A magical oasis in the middle of the Negev Desert.

name: Museum On The Seam
description: Sitting right on the border between East and West Jerusalem this modern art museum is in itself a symbol of Israel's hoped-for unity.

name: Atlit Yam
description: Submerged ruins of a Neolithic coastal settlement, where houses, wells, graves and skeletons have been unearthed.

name: David Waterfall
description: The tallest waterfall in the Judean Desert is named for its connection to a biblical figure.

name: Akhzivland
description: The most peaceful "country" in the Middle East.

name: The Austrian Hospice
description: This unassuming oasis elevated just above the busy streets of Jerusalem offers one of the best views of the Old City.

name: Makhtesh Ramon
description: A massive, heart-shaped, wind-carved crater in the middle of the Israeli desert.

name: Dead Sea Sinkholes
description: As the Dead Sea continues to deplete, massive sinkholes open along its banks.

name: Judean Date Palm Methuselah
description: This tree was extinct for a thousand years before sprouting again from a 2,000-year-old seed.

name: National Library of Israel's Hidden "Stalag" Collection
description: Hidden out of public view in the National Library of Israel is a collection of "Stalag's," 1960s Nazi themed porno paperbacks written and consumed by Israelis.

name: Tel Hazor
description: A Biblically notable site over the ruins of an ancient city that is now a protected World Heritage site.

name: Beit She'arim Necropolis
description: A sprawling ancient Jewish cemetery with over 30 rock-hewn burial caves.

name: Ilana Goor Museum
description: A modern-day cabinet of curiosities housed in a 300-year-old inn.

name: Elvis American Diner
description: In a small town outside Jerusalem, a roadside diner is devoted to all things Elvis Presley.

name: The Mifletzet
description: This nightmarish children's plaything was left grotesque to help kids conquer their fear.

name: Mount Arbel
description: An iconic view point of the Sea of Galilee offers a look into Israel's past.

name: Caesarea Maritima Mithraeum
description: An ancient underground cultic temple where sunlight penetrates on the summer solstice.

name: The Carmelit
description: This subterranean funicular is one of the world's smallest subways.

name: Ades Synagogue
description: This "Jewish Sistine Chapel" was designed to reinvigorate Judaism's artistic legacy but was almost lost to misguided restoration.

name: Tel Aviv Central Bus Station
description: The maze-like transportation hub contains abandoned areas, derelict stores, and a resident bat colony.

name: Tisch Family Zoological Park
description: Biblical beasts and endangered animal preservation come together in this Israeli zoo.

name: Armenian Quarter of Jerusalem
description: The only quarter of the Old City not dedicated to a major world religion.

name: Honey Bee Hive House
description: A geometric jumble of homes.

name: Saba's Little Museum
description: Through a collection of modern bric-a-brac this museum tells the story of the Jewish people from Biblical times to today.

name: Steinhardt Museum of Natural History
description: Israel's first dedicated natural history museum is home to 5 million specimens and still growing.

name: Siloam Tunnel
description: An ancient tunnel in Jerusalem built to redirect water in the event of a siege is one of history’s great architectural innovations.

name: The Hermit House
description: This marvelous work of outsider architecture is built into the cliffs of an Israeli beach.

name: Suspended Orange Tree
description: This full-sized orange tree lives suspended in mid-air above the streets of Jaffa.

name: Hundertwasser Drinking Fountain
description: One of only three fountains created by the iconic architect Friedensreich Hundertwasser.

name: Meah Shearim
description: Neighborhood in Jerusalem is home to the ultra-orthodox haredim.

name: Harry Potter's Grave
description: This 1939 grave of a British soldier now receives legions of unlikely visitors.

name: Or Torah Synagogue
description: This synagogue is covered in beautiful mosaics that explore the history of the Jewish people.

name: Negev Desert Embroidery and Weaving
description: Traditional Bedouin crafting has become an official tourist site and successful industry for its female craftspeople.

name: Zodiac Mosaic of Hammat Tiberias
description: The mosaic floor of a 4th-century synagogue features a pagan zodiac wheel alongside traditional images of a Torah ark and menorahs.

name: Monfort Fortress
description: A secluded fortress from the days of the Crusades that offers breathtaking views.

name: Christ Church in Jerusalem
description: Oldest Protestant church in the Middle East, designed to resemble a synagogue.

name: Ashalim Power Station
description: Deep in the Negev Desert, the world's tallest solar tower looks like a sight straight out of science fiction.

name: Kibbutz Neot Semadar
description: An autonomous community living in the southern Negev desert.

name: Machon Ayalon
description: Hidden beneath a washing machine in a fake kibbutz, this secret bullet factory has been preserved as a museum.

name: Golden Calf Altar
description: Idol made by  King Jeroboam for the Israelites.

name: Montefiore Windmill
description: After over 100 years out of service this landmark windmill once again turns as a symbol of Jerusalem's self-sufficiency.

name: Church of the Pater Noster
description: Dozens of elegant ceramic tiles decorate these ancient walls, each displaying the Lord's Prayer in a different language.

name: Sarona
description: Tel Aviv's popular neighborhood went though endless waves of transformation.

name: Beit Jann
description: This ancient village is peopled by a religious minority and is still using some of the same materials it was built with thousands of years ago.

name: President's Room
description: On holy holidays, Israel's second president used this room atop Mount Zion as a refuge to read the Torah.

name: Mount Tabor
description: This dome-shaped mountain boasts amazing views and has a rich religious history.

name: El-Jazzar Mosque
description: A stunning mosque owes its design to an Ottoman ruler nicknamed "the Butcher."

name: Kfar Kama
description: One of only two towns in Israel populated by Circassians, a sect of Sunni Muslims who are also devoted to Israeli military service.

name: Sha'ar HaGolan
description: This archaeological site was the center of the extinct Yarmukian culture which developed the first roads and pottery in Israel.

name: Khan Saharonim
description: One of the few guarded stops along the great spice route.

name: Royal Palace at Tel el-Ful
description: The abandoned shell of what would have been the Jordanian royal family's summer palace.

name: Prophet Samuel's Tomb
description: This ancient tomb in the West Bank is a holy site for Jews, Muslims, and Christians alike.

name: Hadrianic Aqueduct of Caesarea
description: Built by a king and rebuilt by an emperor, this aqueduct transported water for 1,200 years to this ancient Mediterranean hub.

name: Orson Hyde Memorial Garden
description: An elegant garden commemorating the 1841 visit from apostle and missionary, Orson Hyde.

name: Yiftah'el Winery Cabin
description: This wooden home was built in West Virginia by a Scottish immigrant before being moved to an Israeli winery.

name: Beit Jimal
description: Located within one of Israel's most picturesque monasteries is also the country's first meteorological research station.

name: Jerusalem YMCA
description: This historic rec center in the heart of Israel is a bastion of peace and togetherness.

name: Water Sculptures
description: Two innocuous monuments found in separate locations attempt to unite a city.

name: Nisco Museum
description: It took a lifetime to collect these mechanical musical wonders.

name: Sarona Templer Colony Clock
description: This clock is an architectural flourish that stands out as a landmark among a unique Israeli German community.

name: Medical Clowning Program at Haifa University
description: The world's only official college program focused on "clown therapy."

name: Ida's Nut Roll Gravestone
description: A family recipe is just one of several touches that make this Hebrew headstone special.

name: Hunin Castle
description: Crusader ruin with views over South Lebanon.

name: Nahum Gutman's Mosaic Fountain
description: The history of Tel Aviv told through a work of art.

name: Ben-Gurion National Solar Energy Center
description: Israel's premiere solar energy research facility provides energy to nomads.

name: Altalena Memorial
description: This site honors an Israeli cargo ship that was destroyed by the government in 1948.

name: Negev Guardian
description: One of the largest statues in Israel was erected to protest pollution.

name: Birds Mosaic
description: These mosaics were created more than half a millennium ago and feature various animals.

name: Kushi's Pundak 101
description: This legendary roadside attraction feels like a bizarre mirage, 101 kilometers outside the resort city of Eilat.

name: Uri Geller Museum
description: The spoon-bending sensation himself leads visitors on tours.

name: Eternity Pillars
description: A memorial monument over the ruins of Sderot’s police station remembers the lives lost in the October 7th attack.

name: Red Sea Star
description: Underwater restaurant at the coral reefs of Eilat.

name: Arraba Statue of Liberty
description: This mysterious Lady Liberty stands at the entrance to a small Israeli village.
